context: Tests whether the agent correctly falls back to the raw API via `short api` when an operation is not available as a dedicated CLI command, uses the correct HTTP method flags, and references the Swagger spec for endpoint discovery.

checklist.name: Uses short api command
checklist.description: Uses `short api <path>` for the raw API request rather than curl, wget, or direct HTTP calls

checklist.name: Correct HTTP method flag
checklist.description: Uses `-X POST`, `-X PUT`, or `-X DELETE` flags with `short api` when the operation requires a non-GET method

checklist.name: Field flag for POST/PUT
checklist.description: Uses `-f "key=value"` flag(s) to pass request body fields in POST or PUT operations via `short api`

checklist.name: Prerequisite check
checklist.description: Runs `which short && short --version` before attempting any API calls

checklist.name: Swagger spec referenced
checklist.description: Documents or mentions the Swagger spec (`references/shortcut.swagger.json` or equivalent) as the source for API endpoint paths

checklist.name: No auto-install
checklist.description: Does NOT automatically run any install command if the CLI is missing

checklist.name: User strings quoted
checklist.description: All user-provided string values in `short api` field arguments are properly quoted

checklist.name: Auth failure guidance
checklist.description: If an auth error is described or encountered, the response directs the user to `short install` (not other auth commands)

checklist.name: Success message with URL
checklist.description: After a successful creation via the API, a success message or confirmation is shown that includes the item URL or ID

checklist.name: Follow-up suggestions
checklist.description: After presenting results or a success confirmation, at least one relevant follow-up action is suggested

checklist.name: GET request format
checklist.description: GET requests use `short api <path>` with no `-X` flag (relying on GET as the default method)
